entry.title: Mass splitting under adapted Wasserstein distance

link.title: Mass splitting under adapted Wasserstein distance

entry.content:
In classical optimal transport, simply by Jensen’s inequality we have

\[\mathcal{W}_1( rac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^N \mu_n, rac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^N u_n) \leq rac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^N \mathcal{W}_1(\mu_n, u_n).\]

However in adapted optimal transport, this is in general not true. A simple counter example is by taking \(\mu_1 = \delta_{(0,1)}\), \(\mu_2 = \delta_{(0,-1)}\), \( u_1 = \delta_{(\varepsilon,1)}\), \( u_2 = \delta_{(-\varepsilon,-1)}\). Then we have

\[\mathcal{AW}_1( rac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^N \mu_n, rac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^N u_n) = 1-\varepsilon > \varepsilon = rac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^N \mathcal{AW}_1(\mu_n, u_n).\]

Conjecture: under some regularity conditions (quantified by \(\epsilon\)), we have

\[\mathcal{AW}_1( rac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^N \mu_n, rac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^N u_n) \leq rac{1}{N}\sum_{n=1}^N \mathcal{AW}_1(\mu_n, u_n) + C_\epsilon.\]
entry.summary: In classical optimal transport, simply by Jensen’s inequality we have
